issues.title: Enforce frontend patterns for future work
issues.description:
## **1. .cursorrules file (recommended)**

Create a .cursorrules file in the project root. Cursor reads it automatically and applies the rules to all conversations. Include:

* React Query usage for server state
* ARIA label requirements
* Error handling patterns
* Component structure guidelines
* Other audit recommendations

## **2. Extend existing repo rules**

You already have repo-specific rules. Add a section for frontend-specific rules referencing the audit report.

## **3. ESLint rules (for code-level enforcement)**

* eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y for ARIA labels
* Custom rules for React Query usage patterns
* These catch violations during development

## **4. Documentation in standard locations**

* CONTRIBUTING.md in the project root
* docs/FRONTEND_GUIDELINES.md or similar
* Cursor can reference these when explicitly mentioned or in common locations

## **5. TypeScript strict mode**

Enforce type safety rules from the audit via tsconfig.json strict settings.

## **Best approach**

Combine:

* .cursorrules for AI behavior guidance
* ESLint rules for automatic code checking
* Documentation for human reference

The .cursorrules file is the most effective for making Cursor follow these patterns automatically without mentioning them each time.

issues.title: Logger: Implement 'Column Settings' dialog for Items
issues.description:
## Column Settings concept:

This should add 3 levels of settings

* Global (Admin) level so we can configure the default columns for all the users per type of set's protocol.
* User level. if the user config columns differently, it will overide the above level
* Exercise/set level. If the user modify a specific set, it will overide the above levels

The Implementation and design strategy is here: `workout-logger-column-settings-strategy.md`

## TODO:

- [ ] Analyze the strategy document
- [ ] Solve document open questions:
  - [ ] Item/Set storage: Store in routine_items or routine_rounds? (or both?)
  - [ ] Auto-activation override: Should explicit false prevent auto-activation?
  - [ ] Display format: Exact format for inline value concatenation
  - [ ] Item/Set override UI: How should users configure per-item overrides?
- [ ] Solve doubts if any
- [ ] Implement
- [ ] Ask cursor to create tests for it and debug it
- [ ] Test by ourselves

issues.title: Notes to take over Logger
issues.description:
# Benchmarks for Logger page

How to run the benchmark:

```
bash ./docker-compose.dev.sh exec backend bundle exec rails runner scripts/benchmark_workout_logger_quick.rb 2>&1
```

In case it's relevant there is an Analysis document that was created after running the benchmark (done after the optimizations)

1. `workout-logger-performance-benchmark-results.md` — benchmark results with actual data
2. `workout-logger-performance-analysis-and-optimizations.md` — analysis and optimization opportunities
3. `workout-logger-performance-conclusions.md` — conclusions and recommendations

# Cursor Rules proposal for consistency in future pages

DRY principles rules were added to .cursorrules so Cursor always will use these principle when they are relevant. In case it does, it will add references to a document `dry-functionalities-reference.md` and when a new implementation is done, it will check if there is already a functionality to use.

Optimizations have been done for the Logger page to use best practices

I propose to ask Cursor to create a document to integrate all these concepts together and automate it, so we try to reduce the refactoring if Cursor does not use these principles at first.

The prompt could be:

```
extract the principles of the optimization done in Logger, integrate them with the industry best practices and the DRY strategies as well. Let's analyze how can we make that cursor always apply these rules, so we don't have to refactor all the code each time we build a page.
CRITICAL: let's analyze our options, without implementing or coding yet.
CRITICAL: you have to analyze also the cursorrules we already have, so the strategy integrate them without breaking or duplicating them.
```

# Saving all optimization

Document for the saving optimizations that were created for Logger, but might be partially used for other pages, `SAVE_ALL_OPTIMIZATION.md`

issues.title: Hierarchical Documentation Index to Reduce Cursor Context Usage
issues.description:
## Problem

Cursor currently relies on semantic search across documentation and code, which can still pull large and sometimes unnecessary context into prompts.  
This leads to:

* Higher token usage
* Slower responses on large documentation sets
* Less precise application of rules and documentation when topics are mixed

As documentation grows, flat retrieval becomes increasingly inefficient.

## Proposed Idea

Introduce a **hierarchical documentation index (tree-based structure)** that acts as an explicit Table of Contents for all documentation used by Cursor.

Instead of scanning or semantically searching the full documentation set, Cursor would:

1. First consult the **documentation tree**
2. Identify the relevant branches based on the prompt
3. Load only the documentation contained in those branches

This allows Cursor to work with **scoped, topic-relevant context only**.

## Key Concepts

* **Documentation Tree (Node-Based Index)**
  * Root → Sections → Subsections → Documents
  * Each node contains:
    * Title
    * Short semantic summary
    * References to child nodes or documents
* **Guided Retrieval**
  * Query matches are performed first on node summaries
  * Full documents are only loaded if their node is relevant
* **Critical Cursor Rule**
  * Enforce that Cursor must always:
    * Check the documentation tree first
    * Retrieve content only through the tree
  * Prevents uncontrolled context expansion
* **Reduced** `.cursorrules` File
  * Rules can be smaller and more modular
  * Rules are applied only when their corresponding tree branch is selected

# Implementation guide and AI analysis of the idea:

[https://gemini.google.com/share/a21d6b86e07c](https://gemini.google.com/share/a21d6b86e07c)

[https://chatgpt.com/s/t_694bacf91ab08191a7df2f22e17974b7](https://chatgpt.com/s/t_694bacf91ab08191a7df2f22e17974b7)

# New plan to implement it in Cursor

[https://gemini.google.com/share/b157b1af5712](https://gemini.google.com/share/b157b1af5712)

issues.title: Routines model logic
issues.description:
### What needs consideration

Priority 1 (Critical):

1. Status management — when/how status changes
2. Edit/delete protection — prevent editing routines with logged data
3. Date validation — restrictions on past/future dates
4. Data validation — positive numbers, valid ranges

   We should have
   * DONE when the user logs the data after working out (per set in routine_items)
     * when it's marked as DONE, the item/set cannot be modified by any method, but only by the user.
     * the user can modify the values within 1 month, later it cannot be modified.
   * EXPIRED when the date has passed and the set was not logged (set was skipped or user didn't train)
     * it's marked as EXPIRED for reporting purposes as this item was not done
     * but, this set can be login and it's still editable
     * the user or the couch can reassign this item to another date / routine
   * PLANNED when the user has created the routine workout with its rounds and sets/items. Or when it has been assigned from a workout/plan

Priority 2 (Important):

1. Routine name field — should routines have a name?
2. Routine duplication — copy routines to other dates
3. Copy from workout — manual workout-to-routine copy
4. Order management — validation and reordering

Yes, we need name for routines as we can have different routines for the same day (aligned with workouts where we can also have multiple workouts for the same calendar_date)

Yes, we can duplicate routines to other dates as we can do or we should be able to do for workouts

We can create independent routine elements and assign them to a calendar_date, or we can copy them from the workout/plan

User can add more sets or rounds to the routines, also if they have been planned and imported from workout/plan

* the routines that have been modified by the user cannot be overwritten from the workout/plan as we mentioned before when implementing workouts. Verify this is ok.

We have an order of rounds and an order of sets/items inside each round. We can reorder them always.

Priority 3 (Nice to Have):

1. Statistics/aggregation — total volume, PR tracking

   yes, we need to calculate volume, PR, 1RM / 2RM / 3RM per exercise and per user, etc.
2. Completion logic — auto-detect completion

   yes, but what do you suggest here? give me examples
3. Bulk operations — mark multiple as done, etc.

   yes, we should be able to mark all as done once the user has logged the items, but only the items that have been filled up with either reps, calories, time_seconds or distance_meters will be marked as done.

   by the default, kg should show the kg_target that we can get from workout_template_items, or in case we don't have this, it should load the last kg used for the same exercise from the previous time we did it
4. Soft delete — archive instead of hard delete

   yes, let's archive the routines, rounds or items that have been deleted, for 14 days

## Key questions to answer

 1. Should routines have a name field?

    yes, we need this to identify different routines when we assign multiple for the same calendar_date
 2. Should status auto-update to done when data is logged?

    yes, let's do that
 3. How long after a workout can users edit? (24 hours? 1 week? Forever?)

    1 month
 4. Can users create routines for past dates? (for backlogging)

    yes, they can
 5. How far in the future can users create routines?

    3 years
 6. Can users delete routines with logged data, or only archive?

    yes, they can, but they should be asked to confirm, and they will be soft deleted
 7. What happens if a workout is updated after routine is created?

    if the routine has logged items, then it cannot be assigned or modified

    if the routine was not modified yet, the routine should be recreated to mirror the workout it references to
 8. Should order be unique and required?

    it should be unique, and it should dynamically update the other values if we create a new set with an existing order. I don't know if this should be done here or in the frontend. But we need to have an auto incremental list so we can reorganize the order.
 9. What data is required vs optional for an item?

    order

    kg (if not filled up, it will use the default value or it will be 0 in case it does not have a calculated target or default value)

    we need at least one of these: reps, calories, time_seconds or distance_meters

    for the intensity (strength or cardio) it should be optional
10. What makes a routine "complete"?

    when at least 1 set is logged out and the calendar_date of that routine is has passed.

    however we can later modify it as we have talked above

issues.title: Plans -> implement permissions
issues.description:
* individual roles
  - owner
    - all permissions, also it can delete the plan
    - can duplicate the plan to create a new one and be the owner of the new one
  - couches
    - can assign the plan to athletes
    - can duplicate the plan to create a new one and be the owner of the new one
  - reader
    - can see the plan
    - can duplicate the plan to create a new one and be the owner of the new one
  - athlete
    - can be assigned to the plan
    - can duplicate the plan to create a new one and be the owner of the new one
* Team > if a team is assigned, the roles assigned at this team will be granted by inheritance, ie:
  - Admins or Couches of the assigned team will inherit the plan's role: Couch
  - Athletes of the assigned team will inherit the plan's role: Athlete

nodes.title: #19 Plans -> implement permissions

metadata.title: Plans -> implement permissions

issues.title: Plan/block/macrocycle/microcycle/workout -> behavior
issues.description:
* Workouts
  - A workout has to be assigned to calendar_date and to a microcycle
  - We can duplicate a workout and assign it to another date and microcycle
  - We can assign multiple workouts into a microcycle, they can overlap as we can have multiple workouts during the same calendar_date. So they cannot conflict, how to fix this?
  - The calendar_date has to be assigned between the start and end dates of the microcycle
* Microcycle
  - It has a start_date and end_date
  - It has to be assigned to a Macrocycle
  - The start_date and end_date of microcycle, has to fit into the start_date and end_date of the macrocycle
  - We can duplicate it and assign multiple microcycles to a macrocycle, but the microcycles into the same macrocycle cannot be overlapped
  - Microcycles can be minimum 5 days, and maximum 15 days
* Macrocycle
  - The same logic than the Microcycle but Macrocycles will be assigned to a Block.
  - They have to be minimum 15 days and the maximum will depend of the Block's duration they are assigned to.
* Blocks
  - The same logic than Macrocycles, but they will be assigned to a Plan
  - They have to be minimum 15 days and the maximum will depend of the Plan's duration they are assigned to.
* Plans
  - They can last between 3 months and 3 years

nodes.title: #18 Plan/block/macrocycle/microcycle/workout -> behavior

issues.title: membership / user tiers
issues.description:
* Globally the user's accounts will have different memberships with different limitations

  examples
  - athlete free
    - free user
    - max 1 org with 1 team (only for themselves) / cannot invite users to it
    - reporting view limited to max 6 months
    - no custom reporting views
    - limit of workout_templates
    - limit of plan in advance max 6 months
  - athlete pro
    - free included
    - no reporting limits
    - no workout_templates limits
    - no plan limits
  - coach
    - athlete pro included
    - can create and manage 1 organization for himself:
      - create up to 1 teams
      - cannot assign additional coaches
      - assign up to 8 athletes
    - can be assigned to additional organizations (where limitations will not affect its own organization)
  - coach pro
    - athlete pro included
    - can create and manage 1 organization for himself:
      - create up to 5 teams
      - cannot assign additional coaches
      - assign up to 40 athletes
    - can be assigned to additional organizations (where limitations will not affect its own organization)
  - organization
    - reporting unlimited for coaches
    - include 3 coach memberships
    - can create 1 organization and assign:
      - 15 teams
      - 5 coaches (3 included)
      - 120 athletes
  - organization pro
    - reporting unlimited for coaches
    - include 15 coach memberships
    - can create 3 organization and assign:
      - 20 teams x organization
      - 8 coaches x organization
      - 160 athletes x organization

  So in one side we have users with different memberships

  Each type of user/membership own a organization initially.

  Depending of that membership, the organization will have different capacities as described above.

  While a user with for example organization pro membership, will be able to own 3 organizations.

  Let's analyze how to prepare the database for all of this. We don't need to implement the details of the memberships described, but to make the database able to support this logic.

issues.title: Reseach about AI Integration Strategy
issues.description:
* Instead of relying on a proprietary AI agent like OutSystems to *build* the app, you can strategically use AI *within* your custom app to enhance value:
  1. **Cursor Pro:** Use Cursor Pro for accelerating your Rails and React development, which will be your primary time-saver in the early stage.
  2. **Report Generation (Phase 2/3):** The first valuable AI feature could be leveraging an LLM (like Google's Gemini API) to **interpret data and generate narrative reports**. For example, "Generate a summary of the team's performance over the last cycle, highlighting the top 3 performers and suggesting areas for improvement." This is a feature trainers would find highly useful.
  3. **Workout Optimization (Phase 3+):** Later, you could integrate an AI to analyze an athlete's progress and suggest **optimal load adjustments or exercise variations** for their next training block.

  Your current plan is sound. Focusing on the custom Rails/React build gives you the best path to achieving a **complex, custom, and scalable professional product** on a **low initial budget**.

  Would you like to explore **specific database schemas** (tables and relationships) for tracking the complex relationships between **Teams, Cycles, Workouts, and Performance Metrics**?

issues.title: Rails as API Only
issues.description:
Ensure your Rails app is configured as an API-only application (`rails new app_name --api`)

This keeps it lightweight and focused on serving JSON to the React frontend.

* document at docs/RAILS_API_MODE_ANALYSIS.md.

issues.title: Redis -> Setup
issues.description:
Analyze how Redis integrates with the next points:

* **Data Fetching**
  Use tools like **React Query** (installed) to manage the state of your API requests. This handles caching, background refreshing, and error handling, making your app feel faster and more reliable.
* **Asynchronous Processing:** For features like generating extensive PDF reports, mass data imports, or complex comparison analytics, use an asynchronous job processor like **Sidekiq** (a popular Rails gem) to prevent your web server from getting bogged down.

issues.title: Review: Should we keep 1-month limit to edit routines
issues.description:
[https://chatgpt.com/share/693f2a23-f5e4-800e-a90a-de6ce26ee959](https://chatgpt.com/share/693f2a23-f5e4-800e-a90a-de6ce26ee959)

I think we should keep some limit to reduce the back transactions in the database.

Decision taken:

- [ ] Switch Normal users > 2 month limit.
- [ ] Maken Admin users > No limits.
- [ ] Document the rules in Routines

78a16267-b837-48c0-a320-62f0d28f4f31.body:
@misia Router + Agent.md + .cursor/rules have been implemented and migrated from .cursorrules

Created the navigation guide at docs/navigation.md. It includes:

## **What's Included**

1. **Overview** - Explanation of the Router/Catalog system
2. **Router System** - How to use docs/00_ROUTER.md with examples
3. **Catalog System** - How to use docs/01_CATALOG.md for detailed browsing
4. **Rule System** - Explanation of .cursor/rules/\*.mdc files and how they work
5. **AGENTS.md** - What it's for and when to read it
6. **.cursorrules** - Explanation of the bootloader file
7. **Practical Examples** - 4 real-world navigation scenarios:

* Setting up development environment
* Finding React Query patterns
* Finding database backup procedures
* Understanding API endpoints

1. **Adding New Documentation** - Step-by-step guide
2. **Maintenance** - When and how to regenerate Router/Catalog
3. **Tips & Troubleshooting** - Helpful hints and common issues

The guide is:

* Written for humans (developers/users), not AI agents
* Includes practical examples
* Covers all major components of the system
* Added to the Router/Catalog (regenerated with 142 entries)
* Referenced in docs/README.md for easy discovery

Users can start with the Navigation Guide to understand how to navigate the documentation system.

@misia  new plan to implement it in cursor. I will try first with my nixos project. Let's see how it goes there. If we like it we can implement it on the whole project  [https://gemini.google.com/share/b157b1af5712](https://gemini.google.com/share/b157b1af5712)
I used it to reorganize frontend docs. But we can also do this on the project level.
Looks good. And will also help organize docs laying around in the project. Easier to find both for LLMs and humans.
